program_en: B.S.Sc. in Geography and Resource Management
program_chi: 地理與資源管理學社會科學學士
learning_outcomes:
Geography and Resource Management 1. Major
Programme: Knowledge Students will
have a comprehensive understanding of the various theories and concepts
of geography and resource management. Students will
have specialized knowledge of a specific area of concentration in the
field of geography and resource management and developed a capacity to
theorize and generalize knowledge in their area of concentration. Students will
have developed a life-long ability to analyze, explain and reflect on
real-world spatial development and resource management issues covered in
the curriculum. Students will
have developed a capacity to apply various theories and methods of
geography and resource management in their further studies and career
development. Skills Students will
have the ability to apply various quantitative and qualitative
geographical analysis and investigation techniques to find solutions for
practical problems. Students will
have developed essential generic skills such as critical thinking,
problem solving and self-managed learning etc. Students will
have possessed communication, interpersonal and leadership skills that
they can apply in real-world contexts. Values Students will
have embraced the perspective of sustainable development and become
sensitive to their responsibilities with reference to spatial
development and resource management issues. Students will
have developed a capacity to compare, contrast and respect the social,
economic and environmental values of places at different geographical
scales and the significance of local and regional culture in the context
of globalization. 2. Minor
Programme: Upon the completion of the Minor
Programme, students will have demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental
issues related to the theories and methods of geography and resource management,
acquired generic analytical and problem solving skills and developed
sensitivity towards sustainable development issues with sufficient depth and
at a suitable level to allow further study in geography and resource
management subjects or to pursue a related career.
study_scheme:
Geography and Resource Management Geography and
Resource Management Applicable to
students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 72 units
of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
GRMD1401 and any two courses,
offered by two different Programmes/Departments/Schools, chosen from the
following:
ARCH1001, 1002, 1003, COMM1110,
1120, 1150, 1500, DSPS1001, 1003, 1004, ECON1210, 1220, 2011, GLSD1001, GPAD1020,
1076, 1077, PSYC1000, 1630, SOCI1001, 1201, SOSC1001, 1002, 1003, SOWK1001,
1113, 1114, URSP1001, 1002
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  27
GRMD1011, 1403, 2011, 2102, 2105, 3011, 3102, 3301,
4001 (capstone course), 4002 (capstone course)
3.  |  Elective Courses:
(a)  |  Any one of the following courses in Physical Geography:  |  3
GRMD2221, 3205
(b)  |  Any one of the following courses in Human Geography:  |  3
GRMD2303, 2321, 3302
(c)  |  Any one of the following courses in Resource
Management:  |  3
GRMD2402, 3401, 4403
(d)  |  At least nine courses chosen from the GRMD Major course
list as below[a], with at least five courses at 3000 level or above:  |  27
GRMD1003, 1301, 2104, 2106, 2209, 2221, 2292, 2303,
2321, 2401, 2402, 2403, 2501, 3012, 3013, 3014, 3015, 3016, 3017, 3018, 3104,
3106, 3108, 3202, 3203, 3205, 3209, 3224, 3302, 3304, 3305, 3323, 3401,
3402, 3404, 4003, 4004, 4101, 4202, 4203, 4204, 4302, 4403, 4502, 4503, 5110,
URSP2060#, 2800#, 2900#, 3600#, 3800#
Total:  |  72
Concentration Area[b]:
Students may choose to declare one, or up to two concentration
areas by taking at least 12 units in the relevant area(s):
(a)  |  Geo-spatial Data Science
Any four courses from the following, with at least one
course from GRMD2104 or 3106: GRMD2104, 2106, 3104, 3106, 3108, 4101, 4403, 4502, 5110
(b)  |  Physical Environment and Resource Management
Any four courses from the following, with at least one
course from GRMD3202 or 3205: GRMD2209, 2221, 2292, 2401, 2402, 2403, 3202, 3203,
3205, 3209, 3224, 3401, 3404, 4202, 4203, 4204, 4403
(c)  |  Urban and Regional Development
Any four courses from the following, with at least one
course from GRMD2321, 3305 or 3323: GRMD2303, 2321, 2501, 3302, 3304, 3305, 3323, 3402, 4302,
4503
(d)  |  Smart Sustainable Cities
GRMD2501, and at least three courses from the
following: GRMD3106, 3203, 4502, 4503, URSP2060#, 2800#, 2900#,
3600#, 3800#
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  GRMD courses at 2000 and above level as well as those
labeled as # will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  The nine courses should not include
courses that have been used to fulfill the requirements in 3(a) to (c).
[b]  |  The courses to be used to fulfill the requirements of
concentration areas should not include courses that have been used to
fulfill the requirements in 3(a) to (c).
